_instructions.description: This file contains all articles for the Simply Nerdy website.
_instructions.howToAdd: To add a new article, copy the template below and add it to the 'posts' array. Posts are displayed in reverse chronological order (newest first).

template.title: Your Article Title
template.category: One of: Games, Books, Movies, TV Shows, Music
template.excerpt: A brief 1-2 sentence summary that appears on article cards
template.content:
Full HTML content with

paragraphs,

headings,
  • lists, emphasis, bold, etc.
template.author: Simply Nerdy
template.image: Path to featured image in /images/articles/ folder (e.g., '/images/articles/article-name.jpg')

contentTips: Use <h4> for main section headings within the article
Use <em> for italic text and <strong> for bold
Keep the content engaging and conversational
Images should be 16:9 aspect ratio, ideally 1600x900px or 1920x1080px
Place article images in the /images/articles/ folder with descriptive filenames
_instructions.tagGuidelines: Use 3-5 relevant tags per post. Tags help with search and discovery.

posts.title: Sonic the Hedgehog 3 Review: The Best of the Trilogy
posts.excerpt: Sonic the Hedgehog 3 is the best entry in the trilogy—a lore-rich, Jim Carrey-powered blast that delivers more of the characters fans actually want to see.
posts.content:
Sonic the Hedgehog 3 had something to prove. The second film raised the bar but stumbled badly in places, and questions lingered about whether the series could keep building. The answer is a resounding yes—this is the best of the three films, and it isn’t particularly close.

More Sonic, Less Filler

One of the most consistent praises from our expanded panel was simple: this movie gives you more of what you actually want. More Sonic, more Tails, more Knuckles, and more story rooted in the actual lore of the franchise. The human characters are still present but wisely given less screen time, and what time they do get is handled well. The film is smarter about where it focuses its attention than either of its predecessors.

For fans who have been with the series, the Easter eggs and callbacks to the games are genuinely rewarding—not thrown in for cheap applause, but woven into the fabric of what the movie is doing. It has that same quality the Mario movie had of feeling like it was made by people who actually love the source material.

Jim Carrey Squared

There’s no way to review this film without talking about Jim Carrey, who delivers one of the most entertainingly unhinged performances the series has seen. If you’re a fan of his comedic energy, this movie is going to give you exactly what you want—possibly more than you were expecting. The man plays off himself with a chemistry that shouldn’t work as well as it does, and several of his scenes had the panel losing it completely.

A Story Worth Telling

Where Sonic 3 really earns its place at the top of the trilogy is in its storytelling ambition. The film delves into legitimate lore that longtime fans will recognize, handling the emotional core of certain character relationships with genuine care. There are sequences here that slow down and let the story breathe in ways that elevate the whole movie.

The action sequences are spectacular—big, kinetic, and satisfying in a Dragon Ball Z kind of way that suits the franchise perfectly. The film knows when to go big and does so with confidence.

A Few Caveats

Not everything lands perfectly. Some character development feels compressed—moments that deserved more runway are wrapped up faster than they should be, and a few emotional beats don’t quite hit as hard as they could have given more connective tissue. The film is also occasionally content to let a wink to the audience substitute for genuine storytelling, which works better in some moments than others.

Keanu Reeves voices the new central character with solid results, though a couple of line deliveries feel a touch flat in more emotional moments. He clearly did his homework on the character’s history, and it shows—the performance just has a little room to grow.

Final Score

9/10

Sonic the Hedgehog 3 is a genuinely great blockbuster that rewards franchise fans while remaining accessible to newcomers. It’s funny, emotionally resonant in the moments that count, and packed with the kind of lore-driven storytelling the series has been building toward. Go see it.
posts.author: Simply Nerdy
